description: Show a smart PWA install banner on mobile browsers — native install prompt on Android, manual share instructions on iOS Safari — with 7-day dismissal persistence and standalone detection

userStories.title: Dismissal Persistence and Standalone Detection Hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a custom hook that manages install banner visibility logic so the banner component stays clean and the logic is reusable.
acceptanceCriteria: A custom hook (usePWAInstall) encapsulates all install banner logic in src/hooks/usePWAInstall.js
The hook captures and stores the beforeinstallprompt event on window (for Android/Chromium browsers)
The hook detects iOS Safari via user agent — check for Safari in UA but NOT Chrome/CriOS/FxiOS (Chrome/Firefox on iOS cannot install PWAs)
The hook checks display-mode: standalone via window.matchMedia AND navigator.standalone (iOS) to detect already-installed state
The hook reads/writes a localStorage key (pwa-install-dismissed) with a timestamp value to track dismissal with a 7-day TTL

The hook exposes: { showBanner, platform, promptInstall, dismissBanner } where showBanner is boolean, platform is 'android' | 'ios' | null, promptInstall triggers the native prompt (Android only, no-op on iOS), dismissBanner hides and persists for 7 days
The hook listens for the appinstalled event and auto-hides the banner plus clears the stored prompt
The hook only activates on mobile viewports (<=700px) — uses the existing useIsMobile hook
showBanner returns false when: app is in standalone mode, OR dismissal is within 7-day window, OR platform is null (not Android or iOS Safari), OR viewport is desktop
Lint passes
userStories.notes: This hook must be built first — both banner stories (US-002, US-003) depend on it. The hook should be imported in App.jsx or wherever the banner component is rendered.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Platform detection logic, localStorage TTL, and event listener lifecycle require careful handling

userStories.title: Install Banner for Android / Chromium Browsers
userStories.description: As a mobile user on Android Chrome, I want to see a banner prompting me to install the app so I can add it to my home screen with one tap.
acceptanceCriteria: On Android Chrome (or any browser that fires beforeinstallprompt), a banner appears at the top of the screen — including on the login screen (before auth)
The banner shows the app icon (from /public/icon-192x192.png or the SVG), a short message (e.g. 'Add ShoppingListAI to your home screen for quick access'), and an 'Install' button
Tapping 'Install' calls promptInstall() from the usePWAInstall hook, which triggers the native browser install dialog
After successful install, the banner disappears and does not show again (detected via appinstalled event or display-mode: standalone on next visit)
The banner has a dismiss/close button (X) that calls dismissBanner() from the hook
After dismissal, the banner does not show again for 7 days
The banner does not show if the app is already running in standalone mode
The banner does not show on desktop viewports (>=701px)
The banner renders at the top of the viewport, pushing content down (not overlaying it) — does not interfere with the bottom tab bar
The banner component uses CSS modules (PWAInstallBanner.module.css) and CSS custom properties for theming
Banner slides in from top on appear, slides out on dismiss (200-250ms animation)
Z-index is above main content but below modals/action sheets (lower than PWAPrompt toast z-index 10000)
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (usePWAInstall hook). The banner component should conditionally render Android-specific content when platform === 'android'. The component file should be PWAInstallBanner.jsx in src/components/.
userStories.testIntensityReason: UI component with conditional rendering, animation, and native browser API interaction

userStories.title: Install Banner for iOS Safari
userStories.description: As a mobile user on iOS Safari, I want to see a banner with instructions on how to add the app to my home screen, since Safari doesn't support automatic install prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: On iOS Safari (detected by usePWAInstall hook — platform === 'ios'), a banner appears at the top of the screen — including on the login screen (before auth)
The banner shows the app icon, a short instructional message (e.g. 'Install this app: tap {share icon} then Add to Home Screen')
The share icon is rendered as an inline SVG matching the iOS share icon (square with an up-arrow) — not an image file or emoji
Tapping the banner itself does nothing (no action available on iOS) — it is purely instructional
The banner has a dismiss/close button (X) that calls dismissBanner() from the hook
After dismissal, the banner does not show again for 7 days
The banner does not show if the app is already running in standalone mode (display-mode: standalone or navigator.standalone === true)
The banner does not show on desktop viewports (>=701px)
The banner does not show on non-Safari iOS browsers (Chrome on iOS, Firefox on iOS) since they cannot install PWAs
The banner renders at the top of the viewport, pushing content down — same positioning and animation as the Android variant
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (usePWAInstall hook). Reuses the same PWAInstallBanner.jsx component from US-002 — conditionally renders iOS-specific content when platform === 'ios'. The iOS share icon SVG should be inline in the component (not a separate file).
userStories.testIntensityReason: iOS-specific UA detection and instructional UI with inline SVG icon
